rationale: Exceptionally well-crafted profile that reads like polished magazine journalism. The chronological structure (morning to evening) provides natural momentum. The prose is clean, direct, and free of filler—every sentence earns its place. Sensory details (copier toner, burnt coffee, sticky notes, granola bar) ground the piece without overwriting. Ruiz's quotes are sharp and revealing ('Mostly it's triage'). The closing text message is a perfect ending—understated and emotionally resonant. The only minor weakness is that since Ruiz and Riverton are fictional, some specifics (112 cases, 47 emails) feel precisely chosen rather than reported, and the piece could have included one or two more granular, surprising details that would distinguish it from a very good template of this genre. But the voice, pacing, and economy are nearly flawless.
